measurements.measurement_reference: Ground floor door ~2.1m, ~28 courses. Second-floor windows ~1.3m, ~18 courses.

brick_analysis.bond_notes: Standard half-lap running bond. Consistent with neighbouring 116 & 118.

colour.description: Red-orange brick, slightly warmer than 116-118. Some variation with scattered darker bricks. Typical residential quality. Summer photo with mostly even lighting.

special_features.description: Flat brick lintels above window openings.
Brick rowlock courses forming window sills.
Metal railing balcony at second floor, similar to neighbouring properties.
Gable dormer with siding cladding above the brick wall line.

condition.notes: 2.5-storey semi-detached pair, very similar in form to 116-118 next door. Brick in good condition. Vegetation and parked cars partially obscure lower facade. The building follows the standard Nassau St residential pattern — brick lower floors, siding gable, simple detailing.

texture_calibration.notes: Summer photo, partial vegetation obstruction. Standard Kensington residential brick — measurements consistent with neighbourhood baseline.
